questions.question: Gases are separated from liquid air by fractional distillation. The boiling points of four gases are shown. Which gas is both monoatomic and a liquid at –200 C? gas boiling point / C A argon –186, B helium –269, C neon –246, D nitrogen     –196

questions.question: Lithium and fluorine react to form lithium fluoride. A student writes three statements about the reaction. 1   Lithium atoms lose an electron when they react. 2   Each fluoride ion has one more electron than a fluorine atom. 3   Lithium fluoride is a mixture of elements. Which statements are correct?, A 1 and 2 only B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 only D  1, 2 and 3 4
questions.text: Lithium and fluorine react to form lithium fluoride. A student writes three statements about the reaction. 1   Lithium atoms lose an electron when they react. 2   Each fluoride ion has one more electron than a fluorine atom. 3   Lithium fluoride is a mixture of elements. Which statements are correct?, A 1 and 2 only B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 only D  1, 2 and 3 4
options: A student writes three statements about the reaction. 1   Lithium atoms lose an electron when they react. 2   Each fluoride ion has one more electron than a fluorine atom. 3   Lithium fluoride is a mixture of elements. Which statements are correct?
A 1 and 2 only B  1 and 3 only C  2 and 3 only D  1, 2 and 3 4

questions.question: Which definition of isotopes is correct? A atoms of the same element that have the same number of electrons and nucleons, B atoms of the same element that have the same number of neutrons and protons, C atoms of the same element that have the same number of protons but a different number of electrons, D atoms of the same element that have the same number of protons but a different number of nucleons
questions.text: Which definition of isotopes is correct? A atoms of the same element that have the same number of electrons and nucleons, B atoms of the same element that have the same number of neutrons and protons, C atoms of the same element that have the same number of protons but a different number of electrons, D atoms of the same element that have the same number of protons but a different number of nucleons
options: A atoms of the same element that have the same number of electrons and nucleons
B atoms of the same element that have the same number of neutrons and protons
C atoms of the same element that have the same number of protons but a different number of electrons
D atoms of the same element that have the same number of protons but a different number of nucleons

questions.question: What is the balanced chemical equation for the reaction between calcium and water? A Ca  +  H 2 O    CaOH  +  H 2, B Ca  +  H 2 O    Ca(OH) 2 +  H 2, C Ca  +  2H 2 O    CaOH  +  H 2, D Ca  +  2H 2 O    Ca(OH) 2 +  H 2
questions.text: What is the balanced chemical equation for the reaction between calcium and water? A Ca  +  H 2 O    CaOH  +  H 2, B Ca  +  H 2 O    Ca(OH) 2 +  H 2, C Ca  +  2H 2 O    CaOH  +  H 2, D Ca  +  2H 2 O    Ca(OH) 2 +  H 2
options: A Ca  +  H 2 O    CaOH  +  H 2
B Ca  +  H 2 O    Ca(OH) 2 +  H 2

questions.text: The diagram shows a section of an overhead power cable. aluminium steel ceramic Which statement explains why a particular substance is used? A Aluminium has a low density and is a good conductor of electricity., B Ceramic is a good conductor of electricity., C Steel can rust in damp air., D Steel is more dense than aluminium.

B Ceramic is a good conductor of electricity.
C Steel can rust in damp air.
D Steel is more dense than aluminium.

questions.question: Three substances are electrolysed using inert electrodes. options: A 1, 2 and 3 B  1 and 2 only C  1 and 3 only D  2 and 3 only 6

A 1, 2 and 3 B  1 and 2 only C 1 only D  2 and 3 only
